title.to: Unified Engine: menu-trigger-correct-state — Triggers that expand additional content should expose their state to assistive technology

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `menu-trigger-correct-state`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state`, `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_success`, `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/menu-trigger-correct-state/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** When components such as accordions or navigation menus do not expose their state, screen reader users may not realize that additional content can be revealed or know whether it is currently visible.
**Remediation advice:** Assign aria-expanded to common triggers, such as <button> or custom select elements to indicate whether their associated content is collapsed (false) or expanded (true). Ensure the value is updated as the state changes.
**Fixtures:** 6 pass, 2 fail

1. An valid env of accessFlow
2. access to the DB/Ekla
preconditions.to:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `menu-trigger-correct-state` examples published at `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_success` and `/engine/menu-trigger-correct-state_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 6 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

postconditions.to:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Aria Label Missuse" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `name-prohibited-no-aria-label`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/name-prohibited-no-aria-label`, `/engine/name-prohibited-no-aria-label_success`, `/engine/name-prohibited-no-aria-label_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/name-prohibited-no-aria-label/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Using aria-label on elements that do not support the attribute can lead to the label being ignored, leaving users without the intended information.
**Remediation advice:** Use a visually hidden element to provide the name or description instead of an aria-label.
**Fixtures:** 21 pass, 16 fail

title.to: Unified Engine: navigation-item-link — List items in a navigation region should not be interactive

description.from: Testing the accessFlow-Unified engine integration, focusing on the rule
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-item-link`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-item-link`, `/engine/navigation-item-link_success`, `/engine/navigation-item-link_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-item-link/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** Adding interactive behavior to <li> elements within a navigation list may prevent screen reader users from identifying the element as actionable, since <li> elements are announced as list items and expected to be static.
**Remediation advice:** In order to maintain the semantic structure of the list, a properly formed <a> element should be nested inside the <li>.
**Fixtures:** 7 pass, 7 fail

title.from: accessflow-engine integration: Misused Nav Tagging / navigation-misuse
title.to: Unified Engine: navigation-misuse — Navigation landmark does not contain key site navigation links

description.from: Testing the behavior of the rule "Misused Nav Tagging" and the unified engine compatability
description.to:
**Engine rule ID:** `navigation-misuse`
**RAIDEN routes:** `/engine/navigation-misuse`, `/engine/navigation-misuse_success`, `/engine/navigation-misuse_failure`
**Atomic tests:** `src/components/pages/engine-rules/navigation-misuse/atomic-tests/`
**Qase suite:** accessFlow-Engine (902)
## Flow Phases
### Phase 1 — RAIDEN Library Review (Steps 1–3)
Validate rule metadata and HTML examples in the Engine Rules Library.
### Phase 2 — Atomic Auditor Validation (Step 4)
Run core-engine atomic audit against each fixture; confirm pass/fail node expectations.
### Phase 3 — AccessFlow Issue Alignment (Step 5)
Scan a page hosting the fixtures; generated issues must match RAIDEN examples.
---
**Rule description:** A navigation landmark should identify a section that contains primary links for moving through the site or page. Using navigation landmarks for minor or secondary link groups makes it harder for screen reader users to locate the page’s key navigation areas.
**Remediation advice:** Use navigation landmarks only for key navigation sections, such as the main site menu, table of contents, breadcrumbs, or pagination. Avoid using them for general link lists, social links, related links, or other secondary link groups, and keep the number of navigation landmarks as limited as practical.
**Fixtures:** 12 pass, 2 fail

preconditions.to:
1. **RAIDEN** Engine Rules Library running (`BASE_URL` from project `.env`).
2. **core-engine-auditor** atomic test runner available for rule validation.
3. **AccessFlow** environment with website scan capability.
4. **Rule** `navigation-misuse` examples published at `/engine/navigation-misuse_success` and `/engine/navigation-misuse_failure`.
5. **Fixture count:** 12 pass, 2 fail atomic HTML files.

postconditions.to:
1. All pass fixtures produce **passed nodes** in atomic audit and no false-positive issues in AccessFlow.
2. All fail fixtures produce **failed nodes** in atomic audit and matching issues in AccessFlow.
3. RAIDEN example pages match atomic fixture HTML and expected outcomes.
